findings.title: Mission-close bundle only machine-checks the final repair spec
findings.rationale: The mission-close bundle is supposed to support terminal judgment over the full locked installer mission, including setup/init, global backups, doctor/restore, lane coverage, and authority repairs. However its machine-bound included_spec_refs contains only reviewer_writeback_authority_enforcement. The earlier active and complete workstreams remain at blueprint revisions 4, 6, 7, and 8, while load_mission_close_spec_ids only includes active non-descoped specs whose blueprint_revision equals the bundle blueprint revision. As a result mission_close_eligibility_findings checks completion and passed review only for the final repair spec, so a clean parent writeback could complete the mission without machine-checking the earlier active completed specs that satisfy the locked done-when criteria and proof matrix.
findings.suggested_next_action: Repair mission-close package/bundle construction so carried-forward active completed specs are included in mission-close eligibility, either by refreshing their blueprint bindings or by loading all active non-descoped mission specs regardless of historical blueprint revision when they are preserved work. Recompile the mission-close bundle and rerun the two mission-close reviewer lanes before recording a terminal outcome.
